verdict_reasoning: GPBAR1 is a classical 7-transmembrane G protein-coupled receptor (GPCR) with documented cell-surface receptor function for bile acids. The NCBI summary explicitly states it 'functions as a cell surface receptor for bile acids' and that ligand binding triggers intracellular signaling and receptor internalization. GPCRs inherently possess extracellular N-terminal and loop regions; GPBAR1's multipass transmembrane topology anchors the protein body stably to the plasma membrane with extracellular loops and binding surfaces accessible from outside the cell.
